title: Dennis and Dee's Mom Is Dead

jokes.text: Well, the knives are real. They're used for magical tricks. But they're real. You know what I mean? They're sharp too.
jokes.setup: Character explains they got knives from a magician, implying they might be trick knives
jokes.punchline: Circular logic explaining real knives used for magic tricks are real and sharp
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing the gang's twisted logic and dangerous impulses

jokes.text: Hey, Dee. Can you get up against the wall? I'm just gonna... Yeah, yeah, yeah. No. You're not throwing knives at me.
jokes.setup: Character casually suggests throwing real knives at Dee
jokes.punchline: Dee's immediate and firm rejection of this insane request
jokes.explanation: Classic Sunny dynamic - casual suggestion of violence treated as normal request

jokes.text: Let's just throw them at her regardless. Start running. 'Cause we're coming. Ten, nine, eight...
jokes.setup: After Dee refuses, they decide to throw knives anyway
jokes.punchline: Countdown like they're launching missiles at their friend
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing how quickly they turn to violence, countdown adds absurd formality

jokes.text: Frank, we're in the middle of something very important! I got news! Your mother is dead!
jokes.setup: Dennis protests being interrupted during something 'very important'
jokes.punchline: The 'important' thing was throwing knives at Dee, interrupted by death news
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of their warped priorities - knife throwing is 'important', death is an interruption

jokes.text: Yeah, right. Nice try. Very funny, Frank. I'm serious this time. She had a botched neck lift. She's as dead as disco.
jokes.setup: Dennis doesn't believe Frank about their mother's death
jokes.punchline: 'Dead as disco' - comparing death to an outdated cultural phenomenon
jokes.explanation: Solid dark humor with memorable phrasing, though the comparison is somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Who wants champagne?
jokes.setup: Just announced his wife's death
jokes.punchline: Immediately shifts to celebrating with champagne
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Frank's complete lack of grief, maximum economy with huge impact

jokes.text: Would now be a good time to say a few words about my wonderfully warm and caring mother? No. Just, you know, get to the reading part. Let's get on with it, man.
jokes.setup: Lawyer asks if Dennis wants to say words about his mother
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic description followed by immediate dismissal - skip the sentiment
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Dennis's complete lack of genuine emotion, only caring about inheritance

jokes.text: Frank, if your fat monkey heart is still beating... Then congratulations.
jokes.setup: Reading what should be a touching message from deceased wife
jokes.punchline: 'Fat monkey heart' - dehumanizing insult in supposedly formal document
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of will reading expectations, memorable insult that reveals character dynamics

jokes.text: A handsome man with a beautiful soul... And a nicer penis.
jokes.setup: Will praising Bruce in flowery language
jokes.punchline: Escalates from soul to penis comparison in formal legal document
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from romantic to crude, subverts formal will reading with intimate insult

jokes.text: Why are you giving it to him? I'm not giving... You see? I'm just reading what's on a will.
jokes.setup: Frank angrily confronts lawyer about giving away money
jokes.punchline: Lawyer's exasperated clarification that he's just reading, not deciding
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man reaction to Frank's stupidity, though not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Because I want to smash his face until he's dead! Killed dead! Killed dead!
jokes.setup: Frank wants to find Bruce
jokes.punchline: Redundant escalation of 'dead' to 'killed dead' repeated for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's rage, repetition adds manic energy

jokes.text: A mistake? We're twins. Yeah. We were born at the same time. What are you talking about?
jokes.setup: Will calls Dee a mistake while Dennis gets the house
jokes.punchline: Their logic that being twins means they can't be mistakes since they were born together
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic showing their inability to process that one twin could be unwanted

jokes.text: Tell that bitch it doesn't make sense! I don't know your mom. Never met your mom. In fact, I'm certainly not speaking to your mom now. Because she's dead.
jokes.setup: Dennis tells lawyer to relay message to dead mother
jokes.punchline: Lawyer's deadpan explanation of why he can't talk to the deceased
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to their insanity, lawyer forced to state the obvious

jokes.text: You tell her she's a goddamn whore! Always been a whore!
jokes.setup: Frank continuing to rant at the lawyer about his dead wife
jokes.punchline: Escalating insults directed at corpse through confused intermediary
jokes.explanation: Frank's character showing through his crude reaction to being screwed over posthumously

jokes.text: Goddamn it! She's taking it into the grave!
jokes.setup: Learning mother wants to be buried with jewelry
jokes.punchline: Treating burial wishes as spiteful theft rather than normal practice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dee's greed disguised as outrage at normal burial practice

jokes.text: I tell you what, you son of a bitch. I am very disappointed in you today. You tell her for me that I will be in touch with her somehow.
jokes.setup: Frank angry at lawyer for reading unfavorable will
jokes.punchline: Threatening to contact his dead wife 'somehow' as if lawyer wronged him
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist threat implying supernatural revenge, shows Frank's complete disconnect from reality

jokes.text: Yeah! Tell her she's a bitch! You know, I didn't give you the house. That's not how this whole situation works. Yeah, you did!
jokes.setup: Dee joining in yelling at lawyer, lawyer tries to explain his role
jokes.punchline: Dennis insisting lawyer gave him house despite clear explanation of how wills work
jokes.explanation: Decent continuation of their inability to understand how inheritance works

jokes.text: I'm gonna dig up her body in the middle of the night and snatch back that jewelry. That's insane! Frank, that woman is buried down there like Mr. T.
jokes.setup: Frank announces grave robbing plan, Dee calls it insane
jokes.punchline: 'Buried like Mr. T' - comparing deceased mother to heavily jeweled 80s icon
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference that perfectly captures the visual while being completely inappropriate for the context

jokes.text: I gotta get to that body while the earth's still loose.
jokes.setup: Frank discussing grave robbing logistics
jokes.punchline: Practical consideration about soil consistency for easier digging
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny pragmatism - Frank treats grave robbing like any DIY project

jokes.text: You call Brucie on the phone and tell him that you want to introduce him to your new fiancé. Who's that? Me.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining plan to get money back from Bruce
jokes.punchline: Frank will pose as his daughter's fiancé in elaborate con
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for cringe comedy - the plan is immediately disturbing on multiple levels

jokes.text: It's her middle school diary, dude. Who gives a shit? This is her diary, man. It's got all her secrets and shit in here!
jokes.setup: Mac found Dee's childhood diary
jokes.punchline: Charlie's excitement about violating privacy versus Mac's dismissal
jokes.explanation: Standard character dynamic showing different approaches to invading privacy

jokes.text: We are not reading Dee's middle school diary to you, bro! Look, we need to start the healing process. I'm devastated over here.
jokes.setup: Mac refuses to read diary, Charlie argues for healing
jokes.punchline: Charlie claiming devastation over death while planning to read embarrassing diary
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Charlie's ability to justify any behavior as emotional processing

jokes.text: The boys are back in town! I have two numbers in my phone. Charlie and Dennis.
jokes.setup: Mac singing about calling 'the boys' for party
jokes.punchline: Dennis reveals he literally has no other friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Mac's enthusiasm with stark reality of their social isolation

jokes.text: Dooley! Call Dooley. Dooley loves a good party! Dooley in the house! Yeah. Dooley killed himself.
jokes.setup: Mac excitedly suggesting calling their friend Dooley for party
jokes.punchline: Dennis flatly reveals Dooley committed suicide
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - perfect setup and devastating punchline delivery, maximum impact with economy

jokes.text: How about, uh... Let's call Stash! What did you do? I set him on fire. Son of a bitch. All right, forget him.
jokes.setup: Suggesting another friend after learning about Dooley's suicide
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually admits to setting Stash on fire, Mac's mild disappointment
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from suicide to arson, casual delivery makes it funnier, Mac treats it like minor inconvenience

jokes.text: Let's get Z-man. I banged his sister. Oh... Sully! He's got a restraining order against me. Plus, you know what? I banged his sister too.
jokes.setup: Continuing to list potential friends to call
jokes.punchline: Pattern emerges - Dennis has sexual history with everyone's sister
jokes.explanation: Good escalating pattern showing Dennis's sexual destructiveness ruined all their friendships

jokes.text: Wait a minute. What are we saying? We got no friends? Looks like it, right? Wow, that hits home.
jokes.setup: After listing all their ruined friendships
jokes.punchline: Slow realization they have completely destroyed their social circle
jokes.explanation: Solid moment of self-awareness that immediately gets rationalized away

jokes.text: If we were to all die suddenly just like my mom did... we would have nobody left to mourn us. We would have no one left behind us to tell of our great adventures and our glorious triumphs.
jokes.setup: Charlie reflecting on their friendless state
jokes.punchline: Describing their terrible behavior as 'great adventures and glorious triumphs'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character delusion - they see their destructive behavior as heroic legacy

jokes.text: How's the ponytail look? It's ridiculous. / I gotta turn into a liberal yahoo just like Bruce.
jokes.setup: Frank asks about his appearance change
jokes.punchline: He's wearing a ponytail to look like a 'liberal yahoo'
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup with Frank's stereotypical idea of liberal appearance

jokes.text: You've gotta stop thinking of me as your dad and start thinking of me as your fiancé, Seamus. Gross.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining their con artist roles
jokes.punchline: Asking daughter to see him romantically, her disgusted response
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe setup for their disturbing father-daughter con game

jokes.text: Al DS? You touch anybody? Well, sure. Hey, man! What kind of shit is that? You just hugged me! Why would you do that?
jokes.setup: Bruce mentions working with AIDS crisis in Uganda
jokes.punchline: Frank's ignorant panic after they just hugged, thinking AIDS spreads casually
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank character moment showing his ignorance and selfishness breaking through con

jokes.text: All of our fortune. She gave it to you. Weird, right? / Yeah. Good news is it's all gonna go to help the less fortunate. Every penny.
jokes.setup: Dee awkwardly mentions the inherited money
jokes.punchline: Bruce plans to give it all to charity, defeating their con
jokes.explanation: Good irony that their target is actually altruistic, undermining their selfish con

jokes.text: Burn these clothes. Tracy, cancel all my appointments. It's time for my daughter and that idiot who raised her to learn a lesson.
jokes.setup: Bruce seeming to fall for their con and wanting to help
jokes.punchline: Reveals he knows exactly who they are and plans counter-con
jokes.explanation: Excellent reversal showing Bruce was aware all along, sets up bigger con

jokes.text: What up? We're three cool guys looking for other cool guys who wanna hang out in our party mansion. Nothing sexual.
jokes.setup: Reading their friend-seeking flyer
jokes.punchline: 'Nothing sexual' disclaimer makes it sound more sexual
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - their attempt to clarify makes it worse, highly quotable

jokes.text: You're not fat, dude. Yeah. You're also not funny. I want somebody who does observational humor.
jokes.setup: Mac claiming to be the funny fat guy of their group
jokes.punchline: Dennis brutally dismissing both Mac's fatness and humor
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis cruelty delivered matter-of-factly, good character dynamic

jokes.text: What about a bicep? A bicep would really do it. Is this a penis? A... penis? No. It's a bicep.
jokes.setup: They design flyer in shape of bicep to attract masculine friends
jokes.punchline: Their bicep drawing looks like male genitalia to everyone who sees it
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy setup and payoff, their obliviousness makes it funnier

jokes.text: I shall use this crossbow to pierce my broken heart. Yeah. All right, dude. Dude, isn't that guy awesome or what? He's like a poet.
jokes.setup: Stranger makes dramatic statement about crossbow and heartbreak
jokes.punchline: Mac's enthusiastic appreciation of clearly unstable behavior as 'poetic'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's poor judgment in friend selection

jokes.text: You knew? You knew that it was a dick? Well, yeah. I thought we changed it. It was always meant to be a bicep. If you want it to be a bicep, it needs more veins.
jokes.setup: Discovering Charlie knew their flyer looked like penis all along
jokes.punchline: Charlie's practical advice about adding veins to make bicep more realistic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - treating penis flyer as serious design problem with technical solution

jokes.text: These are some of my adopted children. Go ahead, guys. This is your new home.
jokes.setup: Bruce returns with what seems like help for their adoption plan
jokes.punchline: Brings actual orphans to test their supposed commitment
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Bruce's counter-con, forces them to confront reality of their lies

jokes.text: Welcome to the party mansion, bitches! Get your asses in here! Where is everybody? Where are all the chicks? Guess what. It's just you and us.
jokes.setup: Mac enthusiastically welcoming college students to 'party mansion'
jokes.punchline: Students expecting normal party, find three desperate older guys
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup showing mismatch between their expectations and reality

jokes.text: You're gonna tell everybody how we're like those guys from Jackass. How we like to smash stuff and shove shit up our asses. You are gonna get so much shit shoved up your ass tonight, four eyes!
jokes.setup: Promising college students will have stories to tell about them
jokes.punchline: Escalating to graphic threats of anal assault presented as fun
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing their complete misunderstanding of how to be fun or memorable

jokes.text: Dear Diary, the dance was a total disaster. I cried myself to sleep again last night. Mom forced me to wear the back brace. And all the school chanted 'aluminum monster!'
jokes.setup: Charlie reading young Dee's private diary entries
jokes.punchline: 'Aluminum monster' - cruel childhood nickname for medical device
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy revealing genuine childhood trauma, memorable nickname

jokes.text: Have you ever been scorned, Charlie? Uh, like all day, every day, dude. Then you should think of this little girl as your soul mate of pain.
jokes.setup: Ernesto asking if Charlie relates to young Dee's pain
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact admission of constant rejection
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlie's self-awareness about his social failures

jokes.text: If we need to adopt a couple of orphans, fine. We'll get the money, and we'll take them back. Can you do that? Is that possible? I don't know. I'm sure they have some kind of a return exchange policy.
jokes.setup: Planning to adopt orphans temporarily for money
jokes.punchline: Treating adoption like retail purchase with return policy
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy showing their complete dehumanization of children

jokes.text: We'll get the money, and we'll take them back. / Can you do that? Is that possible? / I don't know. I'm sure they have some kind of a return exchange policy.
jokes.setup: Dee suggests adopting then returning the orphans
jokes.punchline: Frank treats orphans like returnable merchandise
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark comedy treating human adoption like a store return policy

jokes.text: I need to observe you at your most intimate. I need to observe you two making love.
jokes.setup: Bruce claiming to need to assess their relationship
jokes.punchline: Requesting to watch father-daughter con artists have sex
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment - Bruce pushes their con to most uncomfortable extreme possible

jokes.text: Why are you guys doing this? That's what friends do to each other, bro. No, it isn't! My mom just died. Can you have a little compassion? The least you could do is let us throw your buddy off the roof!
jokes.setup: College student questioning why they're threatening his unconscious friend
jokes.punchline: Charlie's twisted logic that throwing friend off roof shows compassion for grief
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic turning violence into grief support, excellent escalation

jokes.text: Perhaps now would be a good time for you to pleasure yourself for him. Then eventually Seamus will mount you.
jokes.setup: Bruce continuing his 'therapy' session with fake couple
jokes.punchline: Clinical sex instruction between father and daughter
jokes.explanation: Maximum cringe achieved, though somewhat expected escalation

jokes.text: We're saving ourselves! It's all sexual. You know, I'm also a licensed minister. So why don't we just do a wedding? Like do it tomorrow?
jokes.setup: Dee desperately claiming they're waiting for marriage
jokes.punchline: Bruce immediately offers to marry them, eliminating their excuse
jokes.explanation: Excellent trap - Bruce removes every possible escape from their lie

jokes.text: You just married your daughter, Frank. You know who I am? Of course I know who you are. You're the little asswipe who raised my children and turned them into animals, into monsters.
jokes.setup: Dennis pointing out Frank just married his daughter
jokes.punchline: Bruce reveals he knew all along and delivers scathing character assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal and character assessment - Bruce was in control the entire time

jokes.text: I'm gonna take this and give it to the lawyer, and you're gonna lose the house because Frank was not to set foot in the house!
jokes.setup: Bruce filmed their wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Evidence proves Frank violated will conditions, costs them the house
jokes.explanation: Brilliant long con payoff - Bruce used their greed to make them violate inheritance terms

jokes.text: You're the most horrible people alive. Oh, come on. Oh, that's a little bit going overboard. That's a lot. That's a lot to say.
jokes.setup: Bruce delivers harsh but accurate character assessment
jokes.punchline: Gang's mild objection to being called horrible despite overwhelming evidence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: How did they get free? Oh, come on! We were just having a little fun! You guys still wanna be in our crew? I don't think so.
jokes.setup: College students escaped from being held against their will
jokes.punchline: Mac still thinks they might want to be friends after being terrorized
jokes.explanation: Good character obliviousness but somewhat expected given established pattern

jokes.text: Well, at least we still have Ernesto. Yeah, whoops. No, no. He robbed your house. Oh, son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Mac finding silver lining in keeping one 'friend'
jokes.punchline: Ernesto robbed them while they were distracted
jokes.explanation: Good final twist - even their sympathetic 'friend' was using them
